100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 21, 1926, the United States Supreme Court upheld the decision to deny the citizenship of Japanese immigrants, reinforcing racial barriers in U.S. immigration policy. Meanwhile, a significant earthquake struck the region of Kanto in Japan, causing widespread destruction and loss of life. In Europe, tensions rose as Germany faced increasing pressure from Allied nations regarding reparations payments from World War I. These events highlight a world grappling with social, political, and natural upheaval.
